reviews_data.review_text:
Good selection of different "bubble teas" with fruit flavors, sweet flavors, infused teas, cold coffee drinks, but only a small selection of hot drink options. Choices for add-ins include cold or hot boba, flavored jellies, and a few others. Food-wise they have a small dessert case where you can get macarons and a few other sweets, but beyond that all they had for food was Cajun french fries (which were good), or popcorn chicken, which both seemed kind of odd food choices to me for for a place like this. But it's something.
Lots of seating available (though some of the upholstered chairs looked pretty messy, like a child had gone at them with a pen at one point, which is entirely possible...)
It was a fun little place, and I would be happy to go back to try more of their drinks.

I'm so happy to see boba shops so close to me. Boba Cafe does not disappoint.

I got a House Special Banh Mi sandwich. It has ham, pork, meat pate, cilantro, diakon radish, sliced cucumber and sliced carrot on a flaky sub roll. It's about a foot long. A hint of spice, light, and delicious.

I also got a blended red bean bubble tea. It's awesome. I never had it blended before. It's similar to a Frapucino.

I did get a green tea one last time, but with ice. It was delicious but I kept sucking the ice up instead of the pearls. I prefer the blended now.

Service was great both times. Atmosphere is relaxing and tasteful. You can definitely chill here.

Great addition to Matthews.

Okay so I've been here twice now and ordered two bubble milk teas both times, four in total. All four drinks were made incorrectly...
The first time we asked for the tea over ice, they blended the ice in so it was like a smoothie. There were 6 people in line in front of us and it took 20 minutes to get the drinks...
Went again today to order the same thing, one tea with half ice, one tea with extra ice, and they mixed up the drinks after again taking 20 minutes with only two people in front of me... With all the ice in the wrong drink, it only had like 5oz of actual milk tea...

The drinks taste great, but this is pretty poor service when each tea is $6...

The store is pretty small and has three doors with no distinct line so people just sort of crowd the single register which causes confusion.

Hoped this place would be better.

reviews_data.review_text:
Good selection of different "bubble teas" with fruit flavors, sweet flavors, infused teas, cold coffee drinks, but only a small selection of hot drink options. Choices for add-ins include cold or hot boba, flavored jellies, and a few others. Food-wise they have a small dessert case where you can get macarons and a few other sweets, but beyond that all they had for food was Cajun french fries (which were good), or popcorn chicken, which both seemed kind of odd food choices to me for for a place like this. But it's something.
Lots of seating available (though some of the upholstered chairs looked pretty messy, like a child had gone at them with a pen at one point, which is entirely possible...)
It was a fun little place, and I would be happy to go back to try more of their drinks.

They use small ice pieces so if you forget to ask for less ice, you don't have to worry much because the drink to ice ratio is good. Their banh mi special was also decently priced and tasted fine. On one occasion instead of calling out that my order was ready the owner handed my boba directly to me with a straw and he even took the time to shake up my drink so it would taste good. reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Kristin Currier Ludlow, we can’t thank you enough for the kind words about. Your review means a lot to us and lets us know we’re on the right track!
reviews_data.review_text:
I'm so happy to see boba shops so close to me. Boba Cafe does not disappoint.

I got a House Special Banh Mi sandwich. It has ham, pork, meat pate, cilantro, diakon radish, sliced cucumber and sliced carrot on a flaky sub roll. It's about a foot long. A hint of spice, light, and delicious.

I also got a blended red bean bubble tea. It's awesome. I never had it blended before. It's similar to a Frapucino.

I did get a green tea one last time, but with ice. It was delicious but I kept sucking the ice up instead of the pearls. I prefer the blended now.

Service was great both times. Atmosphere is relaxing and tasteful. You can definitely chill here.

Great addition to Matthews.

Okay so I've been here twice now and ordered two bubble milk teas both times, four in total. All four drinks were made incorrectly...
The first time we asked for the tea over ice, they blended the ice in so it was like a smoothie. There were 6 people in line in front of us and it took 20 minutes to get the drinks...
Went again today to order the same thing, one tea with half ice, one tea with extra ice, and they mixed up the drinks after again taking 20 minutes with only two people in front of me... With all the ice in the wrong drink, it only had like 5oz of actual milk tea...

The drinks taste great, but this is pretty poor service when each tea is $6...

The store is pretty small and has three doors with no distinct line so people just sort of crowd the single register which causes confusion.

Hoped this place would be better.

reviews_data.review_text: What a great addition to Mint Hill.  Appreciate a new healthy dining option for lunch. The banh mi on fresh french bread was great. The drink selections were great too. Lots of fruit drinks and tea options. The indoor sitting area was pleasant. A bit too chilly outside when we visited but they do have an outdoor seating area.  We will definitely visit again.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Dear T Quinn, thanks for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry your experience didn’t match your expectations. It was an uncommon instance and we’ll do better in the future.
reviews_data.review_text:
Came to the Grand Opening. Specifically ordered strawberry popping pearls in my daughter's drink. When they delivered the drink, we couldn't see any pearls and asked before we walked away. Were told they were in there.  We took a few sips and took it back up to show that there weren't any pearls.  The staff argued with me that pearls were in there. Refused to even look. They were very rude.

The drinks may have tasted good and the place looks gorgeous, but we can go to another business for better service.

And for clarification, we ordered two drinks. The other clearly had pearls so we do know what they look like.
